classified_facts.rationale: Three-year cumulative return adds context to capital return trend.

classified_facts.label: Group central costs
classified_facts.rationale: Central costs are a minor line item.

classified_facts.label: Employees proud to work at Autotrader
classified_facts.rationale: Employee engagement metric relevant to culture section, though later shown to have declined.

classified_facts.label: Ethnically diverse staff
classified_facts.rationale: Diversity metric, peripheral ESG detail.

classified_facts.label: Monthly visits
classified_facts.rationale: Monthly visits is a key KPI evidencing marketplace scale.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader operates as a marketplace platform connecting buyers and sellers of cars, with a software and data platform that supports profitable retailing from sourcing through to sale.
classified_facts.rationale: Core description of business model and market position.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader maintains a large and highly engaged audience, strong brand trust, and network effects between buyers and sellers. The company's competitive position has strengthened when measured against other automotive marketplaces.
classified_facts.rationale: Competitive positioning statement supports market position narrative.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader includes AI-powered Co-Driver product, continued roll-out of Deal Builder, and launched Buying Signals. The company is using over 50 AI models across operations, combining advanced Large Language Models with proprietary real-time dataset.
classified_facts.rationale: AI product initiatives are key strategic developments.

classified_facts.label: Record numbers of buyers using the platform at 81.7m monthly visits in 2026, up from 81.6m in 2025. Autotrader users spend 6x more time on the platform than all main competitors combined.
classified_facts.rationale: Visit growth and competitive engagement multiple is a strong KPI.

classified_facts.label: The average number of retailer forecourts in the period was down 0.5% to 13,942 in 2026 from 14,013 in 2025.
classified_facts.rationale: Retailer base decline is contextual detail on customer base.

classified_facts.label: More challenging market conditions with increased cost pressure for retailers and manufacturers. Challenging end to the year which impacts growth in both 2026 and 2027.
classified_facts.rationale: Market conditions risk directly affecting growth outlook, headline risk.

classified_facts.label: Rapidly changing technology environment presents competitive risk. While AI will change how many users begin their research, a trusted, up-to-date information source will remain important to maintain market position.
classified_facts.rationale: Technology/competitive risk from AI disruption is significant.

classified_facts.label: Received increased levels of feedback in November on the roll-out of some new products, indicating potential customer dissatisfaction with product changes.
classified_facts.rationale: Customer dissatisfaction with product rollout is a notable execution risk.

classified_facts.label: Three interconnected strategic focus areas: (1) Marketplace - Be the best place to buy and sell a car; (2) Platform - Be the industry's data and technology platform; (3) Digital retailing - Be the enabler for more to be done online. Working responsibly is embedded into everything.
classified_facts.rationale: Core strategic framework, headline strategic priorities.

classified_facts.label: New partnership with the national charity Good Things Foundation and Greater Manchester Combined Authority (GMCA) to tackle digital exclusion. The output will create a five-year roadmap to help shape future policy.
classified_facts.rationale: Digital inclusion partnership is peripheral CSR initiative.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader has a long track record of strong cash generation which it expects to continue. Capital allocation policy focuses on investment in the business supporting growth, while returning approximately one third of net income to shareholders through dividends.
classified_facts.rationale: Capital allocation policy is central to investment case.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader has accelerated its share buyback programme, purchasing 58.5 million shares in the year (6.6% of issued share capital). Over 2026 and 2027, the company expects to return over £1bn to shareholders through combined share buyback and dividend programmes.
classified_facts.rationale: Accelerated buyback and >£1bn return plan is a headline capital return commitment.

classified_facts.label: We are already seeing the application of AI improve the buyer experience, automate retailer tasks and enhance colleague productivity. While AI will change how many users begin their research, buying a car remains a complex, time-consuming journey for a high-value item, where inventory changes daily. We believe a trusted, up-to-date information source will remain an important part of the journey and Autotrader is well placed to continue in this role.
classified_facts.rationale: Management view on AI's role supports strategic positioning discussion.

classified_facts.label: Delivered growth in revenue and profit this year, despite more challenging market conditions, with continued strong demand and constrained supply, added to by increased cost pressure for retailers and manufacturers. The Board recognises there has been a challenging end to the year which impacts growth in both 2026 and 2027, although we have seen a gradual increase in some of our core metrics as we've entered the new financial year.
classified_facts.rationale: Direct management commentary on growth and market challenges, headline quote.

classified_facts.label: The Board believes the prevailing Autotrader share price does not reflect the Company's underlying fundamentals or long-term prospects. Despite a rapidly changing technology environment, our current competitive position has strengthened, we are adapting our car buying experience to evolve with consumer habits, and we remain comfortable our investment in technology is sufficient to take full advantage of AI.
classified_facts.rationale: Board view on undervaluation is a key investment case quote.

classified_facts.label: Having received increased levels of feedback in November on the roll-out of some of these products, we remain committed to delivering value to customers and will continue to listen and evolve our product offering.
classified_facts.rationale: Commentary on product feedback response, supports risk narrative.

classified_facts.label: In 2027 we currently expect to return £600m to shareholders. This will be through purchasing c.£500m of shares (we will be seeking authority to purchase up to 15% of issued share capital at our AGM), as well as paying a third of net income in dividends. Based on current assumptions, this would increase leverage to c.1.0x.
classified_facts.rationale: Explicit 2027 capital return guidance is a headline forward-looking figure.

classified_facts.label: Average retailer forecourts
classified_facts.rationale: Average retailer forecourts KPI, duplicate of earlier figure.

classified_facts.label: Average revenue per retailer (ARPR)
classified_facts.rationale: ARPR is a useful KPI for retailer monetisation.

classified_facts.label: Average live car listings
classified_facts.rationale: Live listings count is operational detail.

classified_facts.label: Technology and data service calls
classified_facts.rationale: Technology service calls volume is a minor operational statistic.

classified_facts.label: Cross-platform visits
classified_facts.rationale: Duplicate of monthly visits figure already captured.

classified_facts.label: GHG emissions across Scopes 1, 2 and 3
classified_facts.rationale: GHG emissions figure relevant to ESG risk discussion.

classified_facts.label: Women in organisation
classified_facts.rationale: Gender diversity metric, peripheral ESG detail.

classified_facts.label: Ethnically diverse employees
classified_facts.rationale: Duplicate ethnic diversity metric.

classified_facts.label: Employee engagement score
classified_facts.rationale: Engagement score decline is notable for culture section.

classified_facts.label: Deal Builder retailers
classified_facts.rationale: Deal Builder retailer adoption metric supports product growth narrative.

classified_facts.label: Deal Builder live vehicles
classified_facts.rationale: Deal Builder vehicle count, supporting detail.

classified_facts.label: Deal Builder deals with full reservation placed
classified_facts.rationale: Deal Builder deal volume shows product traction.

classified_facts.label: EV share of new car stock
classified_facts.rationale: EV share of new car stock is peripheral market data.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader is 11x larger than its nearest competitor in terms of time on site against a collection of four brands, and 22x larger than the next largest individual brand.
classified_facts.rationale: Quantifies competitive moat via time-on-site multiples.

classified_facts.label: Six times more time is spent on Autotrader than all main competitors combined, with 67% of Autotrader's audience unique to the platform.
classified_facts.rationale: Reinforces competitive engagement data, somewhat repetitive.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader's proprietary dataset is integrated with over 220 technology partners, and the company holds the broadest view of the UK car market with almost 300 people in customer-facing teams using tools to identify operational opportunities and performance metrics.
classified_facts.rationale: Data platform scale and partner integrations support platform strategy.

classified_facts.label: Co-Driver, a generative AI-powered product, automates vehicle description generation and vehicle highlights for retailers, with 86% of retailers having used one of the Co-Driver tools since launch, generating 1.9 million descriptions and 700k smart image re-orders.
classified_facts.rationale: Co-Driver adoption stats show AI product traction, supporting strategy narrative.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader launched Buying Signals in January 2026, a proprietary machine learning product that flags high-intent leads converting at twice the average rate, already featured on over 800k enquiries.
classified_facts.rationale: Buying Signals launch and performance is a notable new product initiative.

classified_facts.label: Deal Builder has been scaled to 6.7k retailers (up from 2.0k in March 2025) with 175k vehicles live at end of March 2026, generating 137k deals with full reservation placed (2025: 49k).
classified_facts.rationale: Deal Builder scaling data is key evidence of digital retailing strategy execution.

classified_facts.label: Over 80% of Autotrader visits are direct (via mobile apps, URL, or brand searches), 13% from organic search, and only 4% from paid web traffic; less than 1% of audience comes from generative AI chat assistants.
classified_facts.rationale: Traffic source breakdown shows resilience to AI search disruption.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader receives approximately 9 million unique visitors monthly with 548 million minutes of activity on site (2025: 557 million), with cross-platform visits averaging 81.7 million per month.
classified_facts.rationale: Additional traffic detail, largely repetitive of visits KPI.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader has fully transitioned to a modern cloud-based delivery and data platform based largely on open-source technologies, with an AI platform allowing quick build, training and roll-out of AI services using a range of foundational Large Language Models.
classified_facts.rationale: Cloud/AI platform infrastructure supports technology strategy narrative.

classified_facts.label: Consumer behaviour is shifting towards conversational chat interfaces powered by LLMs, which could reduce reliance on traditional search and marketplace functionality. However, car buying is described as a high-value, multi-step regulated process where each vehicle is unique and changes daily.
classified_facts.rationale: AI/chat interface disruption risk to marketplace model is significant.

classified_facts.label: Retailer sentiment softened during the year due to difficult cost-related trading conditions and concerns with the speed and nature of the Deal Builder product roll-out, leading to a slight decrease in retailer numbers.
classified_facts.rationale: Retailer sentiment decline and product rollout issues are a material risk.

classified_facts.label: Employee engagement has fallen significantly from 91% to 72% due to restructures, external factors, and a tighter approach to working in the office during a challenging year.
classified_facts.rationale: Sharp employee engagement drop from 91% to 72% is a significant management/people risk.

classified_facts.label: GHG emissions increased 55% to 144.1k tonnes CO₂, driven by one-off capital expenditure on the new office and an increase in the number of vehicles taken on balance sheet by Autorama.
classified_facts.rationale: GHG emissions increase is a notable ESG risk data point.

classified_facts.label: Develop and scale AI-enabled products and services using proprietary models and LLMs, including Co-Driver for automated vehicle descriptions and highlights, Buying Signals for high-intent lead identification, and ChatGPT integration via Model Context Protocol with plans for additional integrations.
classified_facts.rationale: AI product roadmap detail supports strategic technology narrative.

classified_facts.label: Continue investing in cloud-based infrastructure, open-source technologies, and a modern AI platform to maintain competitive advantage while building new features quickly on stable foundations.
classified_facts.rationale: General technology infrastructure statement, less distinctive.

classified_facts.label: Scale Deal Builder to additional retailers with custom integrations for large customers, focusing on improving retailer conversion and reducing manual effort through AI-powered automation.
classified_facts.rationale: Deal Builder scaling plan, somewhat repetitive of other product facts.

classified_facts.label: Expand technology and data services to finance, insurance, and manufacturer partners through APIs and integrations, with the goal of embedding Autotrader data into decision-making processes across the broader automotive industry.
classified_facts.rationale: Expansion into finance/insurance data services is a notable growth avenue.

classified_facts.label: Integrate leasing offers into core Autotrader search experience and build a scalable and robust checkout journey to enable growth in the personal leasing channel (PCH) as volumes return.
classified_facts.rationale: Leasing product integration is a niche strategic initiative.

classified_facts.label: Achieve net zero emissions by 2040 and halve carbon emissions by 2030, with targets validated by the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi).
classified_facts.rationale: Net zero targets are standard ESG commitments, peripheral.

classified_facts.label: Group operating profit is expected to be £395m – £415m for financial year 2027, with an accelerated level of share buybacks supporting at least high single digit Basic EPS growth.
classified_facts.rationale: FY2027 operating profit guidance is a headline forward-looking figure.

classified_facts.label: Management expects to maintain Group operating profit margins (excluding Vehicle & Accessory sales) at current levels and expects Autotrader revenue to grow in the second half of 2026 following improvements in paid stock and package penetration.
classified_facts.rationale: Margin and revenue growth outlook is important supporting guidance.

classified_facts.label: Average retailer forecourts are expected to be 1-2% lower for the full year, with stock expected to recover from current levels to minus £30-40 over the next 12 months.
classified_facts.rationale: Retailer forecourt outlook is supporting detail on customer base trend.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader is committed to delivering more, higher quality enquiries that convert at double the rate into sales, which has always been at the core of the Deal Builder proposition and drives long-term value for buyers, retailers, and Autotrader.
classified_facts.rationale: Reiterates Deal Builder value proposition, supporting strategic commentary.

classified_facts.label: AI capability over the next few years is expected to be as significant a technology transition as the internet, mobile, big data, cloud and machine-learning combined, with Autotrader well positioned to provide richer experiences for buyers and better tooling for retailers.
classified_facts.rationale: Management's framing of AI as major technology transition is a key strategic statement.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader expects Autorama to make a small profit for the year, with Commission & Ancillary revenue growing 8-12% and Vehicle & Accessory sales of approximately £40m.
classified_facts.rationale: Autorama outlook provides segment-level guidance detail.

classified_facts.label: Co-Driver reduces the average time taken to list a vehicle from 28 minutes to 5 minutes, which is significant when multiplied across more than 340,000 vehicles uploaded monthly by retailers.
classified_facts.rationale: Concrete productivity improvement stat from Co-Driver supports AI strategy narrative.

classified_facts.label: Deal Builder retailers
classified_facts.rationale: Duplicate Deal Builder retailer count already covered.

classified_facts.label: Deal Builder live vehicles
classified_facts.rationale: Duplicate Deal Builder vehicle count already covered.

classified_facts.label: Employee pride in working for Autotrader
classified_facts.rationale: Duplicate employee engagement figure.

classified_facts.label: Used car transactions
classified_facts.rationale: Used car transaction volume is useful market-size context.

classified_facts.label: Average used vehicle price
classified_facts.rationale: Average used vehicle price is peripheral market data.

classified_facts.label: New car registrations
classified_facts.rationale: Market-level KPI on new car registrations provides context but is not company-specific.

classified_facts.label: Electric vehicle share of new car registrations
classified_facts.rationale: EV share trend relevant to market/strategy discussion but secondary detail.

classified_facts.label: UK car parc
classified_facts.rationale: UK car parc size is background market data, peripheral to company performance.

classified_facts.label: Platform visits
classified_facts.rationale: Platform visits of ~1bn is a key engagement KPI demonstrating market leadership.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader is the UK's largest automotive marketplace and leading digital platform for the automotive industry, with an industry-leading technology and data platform increasingly used across the automotive industry.
classified_facts.rationale: Core positioning statement defining Auto Trader as UK's largest automotive marketplace - headline business description.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader launched Deal Builder, with 6.7k retailers on the product and 175k vehicles live as of March 2026, and has launched Buying Signals which uses AI-driven signals to identify high-intent leads and buyer preferences.
classified_facts.rationale: Deal Builder and Buying Signals are key product initiatives with specific adoption metrics.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader recorded nearly one billion platform visits in the financial year, helping partners' stock turn over at a highly profitable pace.
classified_facts.rationale: Reiterates platform visits stat with commentary on stock turnover benefit for partners.

classified_facts.label: The used car market concluded 2026 with 7.7 million transactions, representing steady 1% year-on-year growth, with average used vehicle prices at £17,597 across financial year 2026.
classified_facts.rationale: Used car market size and pricing data provide important market context for the company's core market.

classified_facts.label: New car registrations grew 5% over the past 12 months to 2.1 million (2025: 2.0 million), with electric vehicles achieving a record 23.4% share.
classified_facts.rationale: New car registration growth and EV share detail, useful market colour.

classified_facts.label: The market faces structural supply headwinds as 2.5 million 'lost' pandemic registrations age through the vehicle parc, resulting in a forecasted 17% drop in availability of 5-7-year-old vehicles over the next two years.
classified_facts.rationale: Structural supply headwind forecasting a significant drop in used vehicle availability is a material market risk.

classified_facts.label: Mixed messaging from Government regarding electric vehicles is risking interest in EVs, as a newly proposed pay per mile EV tax (Electric Vehicle Excise Duty) threatens to erode the cost savings of running an EV.
classified_facts.rationale: EV policy uncertainty is a relevant but secondary regulatory risk.

classified_facts.label: In November 2025, Autotrader received negative sentiment around Deal Builder products, requiring customer listening sessions, product variations, testing of front-end consumer journeys, and language changes around 'Deals'.
classified_facts.rationale: Negative sentiment around Deal Builder is a material operational/product risk affecting retailer adoption.

classified_facts.label: Digital retailing strategy to be the enabler for more to be done online, with focus on delivering more, higher quality enquiries that convert at high rates for customers. Core proposition is Deal Builder, with Buying Signals launched to identify high-intent leads and buyer preferences.
classified_facts.rationale: Digital retailing strategy and Deal Builder/Buying Signals are core strategic initiatives.

classified_facts.label: ESG strategy underpinned by purpose of 'Driving Change Together. Responsibly', focused on being a responsible business. Established Responsible Change Forum to drive forward ESG priorities.
classified_facts.rationale: ESG strategy statement is fairly generic/boilerplate purpose language.

classified_facts.label: Continuing to bring more of the car buying journey online, leveraging unparalleled data and AI-powered tools to help retailers navigate supply gaps and fully capitalise on strong levels of buyer engagement.
classified_facts.rationale: General AI/data strategy statement, supportive but somewhat generic.

classified_facts.label: Developing leaders through investment in tools and resources and 'Leader as Coach' programme. Launched first T-Level placement and expanded L4 software engineering apprenticeship.
classified_facts.rationale: Leadership development programme is minor people/culture detail.

classified_facts.label: The UK car market is moving closer to 10 million total transactions, effectively returning the industry to its pre-pandemic scale for the first time since 2019. The combined new and used car market demonstrates exceptional resilience with underlying drivers of a growing population, expanding parc and increasing transaction volumes remaining firmly in place.
classified_facts.rationale: Market outlook returning to pre-pandemic scale is an important trend for investment thesis.

classified_facts.label: Retailer gross margins remain consistently stable between 9% and 11%. As vehicle values rise, higher vehicle prices reliably translate into higher absolute gross profits, underpinning robust, growing profit for the industry.
classified_facts.rationale: Retailer gross margin stability underpins the value proposition and pricing power narrative.

classified_facts.label: Management believes that delivering more, higher quality enquiries that convert at high rates will drive long-term value for buyers, retailer customers and Autotrader, while being difficult for others to replicate due to the data and technology moat.
classified_facts.rationale: Management commentary on competitive moat, useful but qualitative/generic.

classified_facts.label: The company's purpose is Driving Change Together, focused on driving change in an industry that needs to evolve to adapt to changing consumer needs and the mainstream adoption of electric vehicles.
classified_facts.rationale: Purpose statement is largely boilerplate ESG/mission language.

classified_facts.label: The business model brings together a diverse set of stakeholders including consumers, customers (retailers, manufacturers and other customers), suppliers and partners, underpinned by a collaborative, people-led culture.
classified_facts.rationale: Generic stakeholder description, boilerplate.

classified_facts.label: Committed to acting responsibly with focus on diversity and inclusion, environmental sustainability practices, and maintaining high ethical conduct standards, trust and transparency.
classified_facts.rationale: Generic ESG/D&I commitment language, boilerplate.

classified_facts.label: Share Buyback and Cancellation
classified_facts.rationale: Share buyback and cancellation figure is directly relevant to capital allocation/shareholder returns.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader operates across three main segments: Autotrader (core marketplace), Trade revenue (Retailer, Platform, Home Trader and other smaller revenue streams), and Autorama (Vehicle & Accessory sales and Commission & Ancillary revenue).
classified_facts.rationale: Segment structure description provides useful business model context.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader accounts for over 75% of all minutes spent on automotive classified sites and is 11x larger than its nearest competitor.
classified_facts.rationale: Market dominance stat (75% of minutes, 11x larger than nearest competitor) is a headline competitive positioning fact.

classified_facts.label: The business model operates on a per-retailer subscription basis, with pricing delivered through annual product and pricing events.
classified_facts.rationale: Subscription/pricing model description is generic operating detail.

classified_facts.label: Stock had a negative contribution in the year due to a small decline in retailers and some customers moderating spend in the fourth quarter, indicating sensitivity to macroeconomic conditions and customer spending patterns.
classified_facts.rationale: Retailer decline and spend moderation highlights sensitivity to macro conditions, a material risk.

classified_facts.label: Deal Builder product roll-out experienced pushback from retailers related to the speed and nature of implementation, affecting retailer base growth.
classified_facts.rationale: Deal Builder rollout pushback is a material product/execution risk affecting retailer growth.

classified_facts.label: GHG emissions increased 55% year-on-year to 144.1k tonnes CO2, primarily due to greater volume of vehicles sold by Autorama passing through the balance sheet and capex incurred for Manchester office relocation.
classified_facts.rationale: GHG emissions increase is an ESG detail, peripheral to core financial risks.

classified_facts.label: Marketplace platform strategy focused on attracting car buyers with continued investment in user engagement despite economic uncertainty.
classified_facts.rationale: Generic platform engagement strategy statement, repetitive.

classified_facts.label: Digital retailing capabilities being rolled out through Deal Builder product, though experiencing retailer adoption challenges.
classified_facts.rationale: Deal Builder rollout strategy with noted adoption challenges, contextual.

classified_facts.label: Pricing and product strategy leveraging annual packaging events with expansion of ancillary products such as Co-Driver to drive average revenue per retailer growth.
classified_facts.rationale: Pricing/packaging strategy and Co-Driver ancillary product are key ARPR growth drivers.

classified_facts.label: Workforce optimization with FTE levels stabilizing at 1,244 (down 2% from prior year) to match current resource requirements.
classified_facts.rationale: Workforce optimization detail is a minor operational note.

classified_facts.label: Group revenue increased by 4% year-on-year, with ARPR grew 5%/£141 in the year to £2,995, driven by product and pricing levers. Stock had a negative contribution in the year due to a small decline in retailers and some customers moderating spend in the fourth quarter.
classified_facts.rationale: Key management commentary on revenue growth drivers (ARPR growth, stock decline) directly cited in memo.

classified_facts.label: Group operating profit increased by 4% to £392.7m, reflecting the increase in revenue and reduced losses of £2.0m in Autorama.
classified_facts.rationale: Operating profit growth and Autorama loss reduction is headline profitability commentary.

classified_facts.label: Average monthly cross-platform visits marginally increased to 81.7 million per month, with continued high levels of demand from car buyers despite economic uncertainty underpinning strong visit numbers.
classified_facts.rationale: Platform engagement commentary is supporting context, somewhat repetitive.

classified_facts.label: The average number of retailer forecourts decreased 1% to 13,942, reflecting difficult trading conditions and pushback related to the speed and nature of the Deal Builder product roll-out.
classified_facts.rationale: Explains retailer base decline tied to Deal Builder rollout issues, important risk/performance link.

classified_facts.label: Employee engagement (proud to work at Autotrader) reduced significantly to 72% in FY2026 from 91% in FY2025. Management remains committed to improving this measure over the next 12 months.
classified_facts.rationale: Significant drop in employee engagement score is a notable management/culture metric.

classified_facts.rationale: Used car stock KPI provides operational context.

classified_facts.label: Average New Car Stock (Autotrader)
classified_facts.rationale: New car stock KPI, minor relative to used car focus.

classified_facts.label: Average Live Cars Advertised (Autotrader)
classified_facts.rationale: Live cars advertised is a key marketplace KPI.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader operates a multi-revenue platform including Retailer, Home Trader, Consumer Services (Private and Motoring Services), and Manufacturer & Agency segments, with Trade revenue comprising Retailer, Home Trader and other streams.
classified_facts.rationale: Describes core revenue segmentation of the business model.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader platform had 451,000 average live cars advertised in 2026 (up from 449,000 in 2025), with 13,942 retailer forecourts (down from 14,013 in 2025).
classified_facts.rationale: Key marketplace scale metrics with YoY comparison.

classified_facts.label: Autorama delivered 8,056 vehicles in 2026 (5,302 cars, 2,520 vans, 234 pickups) with 3,804 deliveries from Autotrader (predominantly cars), more than tripling Autotrader-related deliveries from 976 units in 2025.
classified_facts.rationale: Autorama delivery volumes show subsidiary growth trajectory.

classified_facts.label: The Group employed an average of 1,138 full-time equivalent employees in 2026 (2025: 1,140), with Autorama employing 106 FTEs on average (2025: 127).
classified_facts.rationale: Employee headcount detail, minor context.

classified_facts.label: From financial year 2027, Autorama will operate and be reported as a single operating segment with the rest of the Autotrader Group, following more than half of all leasing transactions being delivered through the Autotrader platform in the second half of 2026.
classified_facts.rationale: Segment reporting change signals integration of Autorama into core business, strategically relevant.

classified_facts.label: For financial year 2027, we expect revenue to continue the acceleration of share buybacks, purchasing c.£500m. We expect marketing to increase in financial year 2027. Share-based payments are expected to be £14m in 2027.
classified_facts.rationale: FY2027 outlook on accelerated buybacks (~£500m) is highly relevant to capital allocation thesis.

classified_facts.label: ARPR growth was driven by the product and price levers, with a negative contribution from the stock lever. Price contributed £117 to ARPR growth driven by the 1 April 2025 pricing and product event combining a like-for-like price increase with additional products.
classified_facts.rationale: Explains ARPR growth drivers, key revenue driver commentary.

classified_facts.label: From November 2025, prompted by the speed and nature of our Deal Builder product and reflecting more difficult trading conditions, a number of retailers reduced the number of vehicles advertised on the platform, contributing to lower paid stock levels.
classified_facts.rationale: Retailer stock reduction due to product rollout and trading conditions is a notable risk/headwind.

classified_facts.label: The Directors consider the Group to have sufficient resources to continue as a going concern given its robust balance sheet, flexible liquidity position, and a Syndicated RCF which has recently increased to £300m and is committed until February 2030.
classified_facts.rationale: Going concern and liquidity statement, standard but includes RCF increase detail relevant to balance sheet.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader has the largest and most engaged automotive audience in the UK, with external measures confirming this position.
classified_facts.rationale: Market leadership claim is central to investment case/moat narrative.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader is the largest and most trusted automotive classified brand in the UK according to company research.
classified_facts.rationale: Reinforces brand trust/market position, somewhat repetitive.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader has launched Deal Builder, Buying Signals, and an AI platform for quickly productionising AI products.
classified_facts.rationale: Key product initiatives including Deal Builder and AI platform, central to strategy.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader's customers include retailers and manufacturers, with exposure to both new and used car markets.
classified_facts.rationale: General customer base description, low specificity.

classified_facts.label: In January 2026, Autotrader moved to a new, state-of-the-art office in Manchester with increased capacity and improved facilities.
classified_facts.rationale: Office move is minor operational detail.

classified_facts.label: Exposure to global macro events could result in customers being unable to trade, leading to loss of revenue, stock, audience and market share. Geo-political instability remains high with potential knock-on impacts from supply chain disruption affecting used car stock pipeline and affordability.
classified_facts.rationale: Macro/geopolitical risk affecting customer trading is a key disclosed risk.

classified_facts.label: Adverse changes in supply versus demand for new/used cars will affect retailer profitability. Higher operating costs and interest rates could affect retailer profitability and reduce their advertising spend with Autotrader. This year saw a tough trading environment for customers with higher operating costs and interest rates affecting profitability.
classified_facts.rationale: Retailer profitability risk tied to actual tough trading conditions this year, directly relevant.

classified_facts.label: The Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act came into force during the financial year placing new obligations around consumer protections. In March 2026 the CMA announced an investigation into online reviews across multiple companies including Autotrader and Feefo, still in early stages.
classified_facts.rationale: Regulatory investigation (CMA) is a specific, notable risk item.

classified_facts.label: Emergence of agentic AI presents both opportunity and disruption risk. Risks include AI being used to disintermediate marketplaces and AI being used to direct consumers directly to adverts, negating the need for retailers to purchase prominence.
classified_facts.rationale: AI disintermediation risk is a key emerging strategic risk.

classified_facts.label: Amazon is expected to launch Amazon Autos in the UK, presenting a new competitive threat from a large technology organization operating in the automotive sector.
classified_facts.rationale: Amazon Autos entry is a significant named competitive threat.

classified_facts.label: As a digital business relying on IT infrastructure, a disruptive cyber security or business continuity incident could lead to system downtime. Additionally, as AI models become more sophisticated, there is a risk they could be used by bad actors to discover and exploit zero-day vulnerabilities.
classified_facts.rationale: Generic cyber security risk disclosure.

classified_facts.label: Employee engagement declined from 91% to 72%. Factors affecting employee morale include restructuring during FY26, recent fall in share price, and negative sentiment from retailers regarding Deal Builder scaling.
classified_facts.rationale: Sharp employee engagement decline (91% to 72%) is a striking, headline-worthy risk/management issue.

classified_facts.label: Marketplace Platform - core strategic priority focusing on strengthening the marketplace with investments in product development, brand, and customer engagement.
classified_facts.rationale: Core strategic priority description, somewhat generic.

classified_facts.label: Digital Retailing - strategic priority to expand online buying experience and new car offerings on the platform.
classified_facts.rationale: Digital retailing priority, relevant but generic.

classified_facts.label: Working Responsibly - strategic priority encompassing responsible business practices, brand protection, and responsible innovation.
classified_facts.rationale: Working responsibly priority is largely ESG boilerplate.

classified_facts.label: Scaling of new car advertising - ambitions to grow in new car market and work with OEMs to enable them to advertise new car pipeline stock on the platform.
classified_facts.rationale: New car advertising scaling is a key growth initiative.

classified_facts.label: Scaling of Leasing and related products - expansion of product offerings referenced in compliance context as part of ambitions alongside new car.
classified_facts.rationale: Leasing scaling mentioned briefly, lower confidence and specificity.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader commenced 'listening' sessions within the business aimed at increasing employee engagement following decline from 91% to 72%.
classified_facts.rationale: Response to engagement decline shows management action on culture issue.

classified_facts.label: New car supply increased in FY26 including strong supply from Chinese manufacturers. This coupled with consistently high consumer demand presents a positive outlook for the used car market in the coming years.
classified_facts.rationale: Market outlook commentary on new car supply and used car demand.

classified_facts.label: The FCA's redress scheme for mis-sold car finance does not impact Autotrader directly. The FCA have stated that the impact on the automotive finance market will be limited, reducing uncertainty compared to prior years.
classified_facts.rationale: Clarifies limited FCA redress scheme impact, reduces regulatory risk uncertainty.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader is evolving its retailer engagement strategy in response to resistance to Deal Builder rollout to ensure customers have a voice in how products are shaped, including through Customer Advisory Groups.
classified_facts.rationale: Explains evolving retailer engagement in response to Deal Builder resistance, important product/strategy narrative.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader launched a new marketing campaign, 'It's Time for Autotrader', designed to target the 25-34 year-old demographic, estimated to reach 96% of this demographic in the UK.
classified_facts.rationale: Marketing campaign detail, peripheral.

classified_facts.label: Autotrader maintains a high Trustpilot rating of 4.7 out of 5 with fraud levels remaining low.
classified_facts.rationale: Trustpilot rating is minor brand metric.

classified_facts.label: Despite competitive and AI disruption risks, the marketplace saw continued record levels of cross-platform visits in the last year.
classified_facts.rationale: Record cross-platform visits supports marketplace strength narrative.

classified_facts.label: The Group is the UK's largest and most engaged automotive marketplace, with the largest volume of in-market car buyers and the most influential website a consumer visits when purchasing a vehicle.
classified_facts.rationale: Core market position statement, central to investment case moat.

classified_facts.label: The Group's Vehicle Check product has been successfully rolled out and the company is now entirely self-sufficient by sourcing all data directly from source, rather than via intermediary organisations.
classified_facts.rationale: Vehicle Check self-sufficiency is a minor operational detail.

classified_facts.label: Retailers can use Autotrader's systems to access services and data, whereas others use third-party technology systems that have been integrated with.
classified_facts.rationale: Generic operational description, low value.

classified_facts.label: In calendar year 2025, EVs made up approximately 23% of all new car registrations, below the 28% target set by the ZEV mandate, with the target for calendar year 2026 increasing to 33%.
classified_facts.rationale: EV registration trend vs ZEV mandate provides useful market context.

classified_facts.label: The shift from internal combustion engines (ICE) to electric vehicles (EVs) could prompt changes to car buyer behaviour. Barriers to mass consumer adoption of EVs include high price compared to ICE equivalents, potential improvements in public transport, changing government policy and reduced incentives such as increasing taxes on EVs, range anxiety, and anxiety over residual value of EVs. Changing and more stringent regulatory requirements could increase cost base.
classified_facts.rationale: EV transition risk factors are relevant market/regulatory context.

classified_facts.label: To achieve strategic objectives, the Group is reliant on partners to support product initiatives, including lenders integrated with the Deal Builder journey. Reliance also exists on third parties for technology infrastructure, vehicle data supply, financing, and fulfilment of some revenue-generating products.
classified_facts.rationale: Third-party dependency risk is fairly generic.

classified_facts.label: A cyber attack affecting the Group or key supplier could lead to data loss, disruption to systems and infrastructure, regulatory fines, and reputational damage. Maximum GDPR fine is 4% of Group revenue.
classified_facts.rationale: Cyber/data risk with GDPR fine cap, somewhat standard disclosure.

classified_facts.label: Increased competition risk includes potential takeover of a competitor by a well-capitalised third party or entry of a new player that could develop superior consumer experience or retailer products including AI-driven tools, potentially disintermedating the marketplace and disrupting market share.
classified_facts.rationale: Competition risk from takeover or new entrant, relevant to competitive moat discussion.

classified_facts.label: Carbon emissions have increased this year owing to the initial costs of the new office and the practice of purchasing vehicles within the Leasing business. Vehicle purchases is the primary source of carbon emissions and this activity will continue in the near future, leading to higher carbon emissions attributable to the Group versus prior years.
classified_facts.rationale: Carbon emissions increase is a minor ESG risk item.

classified_facts.label: The Group aims to continue growing its marketplace through playing a larger role in new car sales and advertising, surfacing the power of artificial intelligence which will enhance existing data products, and moving more of the car buying process online.
classified_facts.rationale: Growth strategy across new cars, AI, and online buying, somewhat generic restatement.

classified_facts.label: Growth in the use of data, being the industry standard platform, and further embedding data into the automotive ecosystem to give buyers and retailers up-to-date insight.
classified_facts.rationale: Data growth strategy, generic and overlapping with other items.

classified_facts.label: Growth in areas outside of advertising, continuing to evolve products and consumer experience to bring more of the car buying process online.
classified_facts.rationale: Growth outside advertising, overlaps with digital retailing priority.

classified_facts.label: The company introduced the Responsible Change Forum to bring senior stakeholders together to shape and oversee ESG strategy.
classified_facts.rationale: ESG governance committee, boilerplate.

classified_facts.label: Price disparity between ICE and EVs has remained a barrier to mass-adoption of EVs despite discounts aimed at stimulating demand. Additional barriers include price inequality between public and private charging, and anxiety around the availability and reliability of public EV charging.
classified_facts.rationale: EV adoption barriers detail, contextual only.

classified_facts.label: There is a pipeline of EVs flowing through to used vehicles, and the company's data indicates that consumer appetite for used EVs is strong.
classified_facts.rationale: Used EV demand commentary, contextual.

classified_facts.label: Despite the ongoing geo-political risks over the last year, the supplier-base has remained resilient with no major disruption or downtime arising from suppliers.
classified_facts.rationale: Supplier resilience statement, boilerplate reassurance.

classified_facts.label: Key assumptions in the financial forecasts include sustained growth in the marketplace as the Group continues to develop its platform and invest in search experience, growth in data usage, growth outside advertising through evolving products and bringing more of the car buying process online, and increase in costs largely through salaries as the Group grows.
classified_facts.rationale: Forecast assumptions detail, mostly technical/boilerplate.

classified_facts.label: The Board has assessed the Group's viability and prospects over a five-year period to March 2031, considering detailed financial forecasts covering customer numbers, stock levels, ARPR, revenue, profit, cash flow and key financial ratios, with stress testing demonstrating the Group would be able to withstand severe scenarios due to significant free cash flow, access to the Syndicated RCF and ability to adjust discretionary share buyback programme.
classified_facts.rationale: Viability statement with stress testing supports resilience narrative for investment case.

classified_facts.label: Dividends paid to Company shareholders

strengths.statement: Autotrader holds a dominant, defensible market position, accounting for over 75% of all minutes spent on automotive classified sites and being 11x larger than its nearest competitor by time on site, with 67% of its audience unique to the platform.
The core Autotrader marketplace generates a very high operating margin of 70.0%, reflecting the asset-light, network-effect-driven nature of the digital marketplace business model.

Cash generation is exceptionally strong, with cash conversion of 106.4% of operating profit, and cash from operations grew 4.6% year-on-year to £418.0m, supporting the group's stated long track record of strong cash generation.
The balance sheet is conservatively levered at 0.3x net debt/EBITDA with a £300m committed RCF until February 2030, providing significant financial flexibility.

Autotrader is investing meaningfully in AI-driven product innovation (Co-Driver, Buying Signals, Deal Builder) which is driving measurable engagement and efficiency gains, such as reducing average vehicle listing time from 28 minutes to 5 minutes and generating high-intent leads converting at twice the average rate.
ARPR growth of 4.9% (£141 increase to £2,995) demonstrates continued pricing power even as forecourt numbers softened, indicating resilient monetisation of the existing retailer base.

risks.statement: Retailer sentiment softened during the year due to difficult cost-related trading conditions and concerns with the speed and nature of the Deal Builder product roll-out, contributing to a slight decrease in retailer numbers.

Employee engagement has fallen significantly from 91% to 72%, driven by restructures, a tighter office-working approach, and negative sentiment tied to Deal Builder scaling and the recent share price decline.
Consumer behaviour is shifting toward conversational AI/LLM interfaces, which could reduce reliance on traditional search and marketplace functionality over time.
Emergence of agentic AI presents disintermediation risk, where AI could direct consumers directly to listings/adverts, negating the need for retailers to purchase prominence on the marketplace.
Amazon is expected to launch Amazon Autos in the UK, introducing a well-capitalised big-tech competitor into the automotive marketplace space.

The CMA announced an investigation in March 2026 into online reviews across multiple companies including Autotrader, an early-stage regulatory risk under the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act.

Structural supply headwinds exist as 2.5 million 'lost' pandemic-era registrations age through the vehicle parc, forecasting a 17% drop in availability of 5-7-year-old vehicles over the next two years, which could constrain used car stock and retailer activity.
Mixed government messaging on EVs, including a newly proposed pay-per-mile EV tax, risks eroding consumer interest in EV adoption, a category increasingly important to the new car market.
GHG emissions increased 55% to 144.1k tonnes CO2, driven by one-off capex on a new office and increased vehicles held on balance sheet by Autorama, raising ESG cost and disclosure scrutiny.

Increased competition risk includes potential takeover of a competitor by a well-capitalised third party, or a new entrant with superior AI-driven consumer/retailer tools that could disintermediate the marketplace.

Autotrader's rapid share buyback pace (58.5m shares, 6.6% of issued capital in FY26, with plans to increase leverage to ~1.0x in FY27) concentrates capital return in equity repurchases, increasing financial leverage and reducing balance sheet flexibility relative to the current near-zero net debt position.

value_drivers.statement: ARPR growth (4.9% YoY, +£141) driven primarily by pricing and product levers (price contributed £117) is the single largest driver of core revenue growth, more than offsetting a negative contribution from declining retailer stock.

Expansion of Deal Builder (scaled from 2.0k to 6.7k retailers, deals with full reservation growing from 49k to 137k) represents a key monetisable digital retailing product that could support future ARPR and engagement growth despite near-term adoption friction.

AI-enabled products (Co-Driver, Buying Signals) are driving productivity and engagement metrics that could underpin future retailer willingness to pay and reduce churn, given 86% of retailers have used Co-Driver tools and Buying Signals convert leads at twice the average rate.

Autorama's transition to a single reporting segment from FY2027, alongside expected margin improvement (moving to a small profit) and 8-12% growth in Commission & Ancillary revenue, could reduce the current margin drag on the group (currently ~0.32pp).

Expansion of data/technology services to finance, insurance and manufacturer partners via APIs represents an underdeveloped monetisation avenue leveraging Autotrader's proprietary dataset and 220+ technology partner integrations.

Continued resilience of the underlying UK car market—approaching 10 million total transactions and returning to pre-pandemic scale—provides a supportive macro backdrop for volume-linked revenue drivers.

bull_case.statement: Autotrader's scale advantage (11x larger than nearest competitor, 75%+ of category time-on-site) and network effects create a durable moat that is difficult for new entrants, including Amazon Autos, to replicate in the near term.

Management's own view that the share price does not reflect underlying fundamentals, combined with an accelerated buyback (£1bn+ planned over FY26-FY27) and high single-digit EPS growth guidance, suggests potential value realization for shareholders even amid near-term growth deceleration.

High cash conversion (106.4%) and low leverage (0.3x net debt/EBITDA) give Autotrader substantial flexibility to continue funding growth investment, buybacks, and dividends simultaneously without balance sheet strain.

Early traction in AI-driven products (Co-Driver, Buying Signals, Deal Builder) positions the company to defend against AI disintermediation risk by embedding itself deeper into the retailer workflow rather than solely as a search destination.

bear_case.statement: Retailer forecourt numbers have declined for a second consecutive period, and management commentary directly links this to retailer pushback on Deal Builder execution and difficult trading conditions — a controllable execution risk that has real revenue consequences (negative stock contribution to ARPR growth).

Structural headwinds in used car supply (17% forecast drop in 5-7-year-old vehicle availability) could constrain retailer inventory and, by extension, Autotrader's addressable stock base and ARPR growth potential over the medium term.

Revenue growth is increasingly dependent on price/product levers rather than volume (stock/retailer count), which may face diminishing returns or retailer resistance if pushed too aggressively, as evidenced by the November 2025 feedback backlash.

Aggressive capital return plans (increasing leverage to ~1.0x in FY27 to fund ~£600m of buybacks and dividends) reduce financial headroom at a time when the business faces multiple emerging competitive and technological disruption risks (Amazon Autos, agentic AI).

Sharp decline in employee engagement (91% to 72%) alongside restructuring and share price weakness could signal organizational strain that risks execution quality on strategic initiatives like Deal Builder and AI roll-out.

diligence_questions.statement: What specific product and process changes has management made in response to the November 2025 Deal Builder feedback, and what leading indicators (e.g., retailer churn, NPS) show whether sentiment has stabilized?
How much of the projected FY2027 leverage increase to ~1.0x net debt/EBITDA is driven by buybacks versus organic capex, and what covenant or RCF headroom exists if operating performance weakens?

What is management's contingency plan if agentic AI or LLM-based search materially reduces direct traffic to the platform, given less than 1% of audience currently comes from generative AI assistants but this could grow quickly?
What is the expected timeline and potential outcome of the CMA's investigation into online reviews involving Autotrader, and what financial or reputational exposure could result?

How dependent is ARPR growth on the annual pricing/packaging event structure, and is there a risk that the size of future price increases becomes constrained by retailer pushback or forecourt attrition?

What specific actions are being taken to rebuild employee engagement from 72%, and is there evidence linking the engagement decline to any observable erosion in product quality, retailer service, or innovation pace?

What is Autorama's path to profitability and long-term strategic rationale now that it will be folded into a single reporting segment from FY2027, and how will investors track its margin drag going forward absent separate disclosure?
